hitchens_question: When did we collectively decide that the most morally serious response to watching villages disappear in Myanmar, banned munitions slaughter civilians in Mali, and entire populations vanish from maps was to allocate ten billion dollars to create a new bureaucracy for cyber warfare while simultaneously fretting about the "fragility" of our own rocket programs? Are we honestly supposed to believe that the pressing humanitarian crises unfolding in real time require less urgent attention than ensuring our defense contractors have a "fresh supply pipeline" for profitable robot warfare? This is the grotesque arithmetic of empire in decline: maximum efficiency in dealing death, complete paralysis when confronting the consequences.
